title: MW source-abbreviation to diachronic layer map (seed)
description: Conservative, reviewable mapping from Monier-Williams <ls> source abbreviations to coarse diachronic layers. This is a SEED dataset, not a finished chronology: only the highest-frequency sources are mapped, the long tail is left unknown, and several assignments are genuine scholarly judgment calls flagged for review (UC-DIA-07). Do not present these layers as exact dates.
assumptions: Layers are coarse buckets for comparison, not dates.
Brahmana, Aranyaka, Upanishadic, Sutra, and Pratisakhya sources are folded into 'vedic' at this resolution.
Dharmasastra (Mn, Yajn, Gaut, Apast) is mapped to 'technical' (sastra), not 'classical'.
Commentators (e.g. Say, Sayana) are left 'unknown' because the cited layer differs from the commentator's own date.
Editorial and catalogue references (ib., W., MW., Cat., Col., Gal.) are not textual witnesses; they are listed under editorialReferences and treated as 'unknown' for chronology.
Every source not present in 'map' resolves to 'unknown' and should enter the source-layer review queue.
